brief:
STORY ARC: discovery — Curiosity, then Discovery, then shared Joy.

Pip the soft blue-grey chick wakes up first in the cozy red barn before Mo (the chick in the straw hat) and Bo (the chick in the round white eyeglasses) are awake. The barn is warm and dim inside, golden first light just starting to slip through the round window. She hears a strange soft humming from outside — the dawn chorus far off. She tiptoes carefully off her straw nest so she doesn't wake the others, her bright orange scarf catching the first warm light.

She peeks through the wooden door. Outside, the sky is turning peachy pink. The sun is rising over the rolling green hills for the first time she's ever really noticed. The old red tractor and the wooden windmill are silhouetted against the warm sky. Her EXTRA-LARGE shiny brown eyes go wide. Decorated painted eggs in pastel colors are scattered in baskets near the porch. Tiny butterflies start moving in the warm air.

She rushes back inside, hopping with excitement, and gently shakes Mo — who pushes his straw hat back into place and blinks. Pip whispers excitedly: 'Friends! Friends! There's a BIG YELLOW LIGHT coming up over the hills!' Mo, calm under the brim of his straw hat, asks softly: 'A big yellow light?'. Bo, half asleep behind her round white glasses, mishears completely — 'BANG-LIGHT? Are we under attack?' — and strikes a dramatic pose with one wing raised, ready to defend the barn. Pip giggles, then Bo giggles back as she sees her own silly pose reflected in the morning light glistening on a decorated egg.

Mo says, gently: 'I think... she means SUNRISE.' He explains in one calm sentence: 'It happens every morning. That's what makes it morning.' Pip absorbs it, then repeats it in her own words: 'So morning IS the light coming back? Every day?' Mo nods, tipping his straw hat.

All three tiptoe out onto the porch to watch the last of the sunrise together. The windmill turns gently. The red barn glows in the golden light. The world looks warmer, slower, almost magical. Mo says softly 'this is the best part of the day, when everything is quiet and new.' Bo whispers back 'and good for snacks.' They all laugh quietly so they don't break the calm.

Pip has her realization: 'If I'm awake first tomorrow... I'll wake YOU up.' They all giggle. The sun is fully up now, painting the windmill and tractor in warm pinks and oranges. They head off toward the barn for breakfast together.
lesson: The sun rising is what makes morning, and morning is best when you share it with your friends.
mood: warm, cozy, golden-hour wonder building to gentle shared joy
angle: Pip the blue-grey chick with orange scarf is the audience surrogate (curious, asks questions). Mo (cream chick with straw hat and yellow bandana) is the gentle explainer (one calm sentence, no lecture). Bo (tan chick with round white eyeglasses) is the comic relief (mishears, dramatic poses, snack joke). Every scene must show emotion on the characters' faces. The red barn, windmill, tractor and decorated eggs must appear in the background of multiple scenes to anchor the brand identity from the channel banner.
hook: EXTREME CLOSE-UP on Pip's soft blue-grey face in the dim barn, barely lit by pre-dawn warm light catching the bright orange scarf around her neck. Her EXTRA-LARGE shiny brown eyes snap open from sleep. We hear a soft humming from outside (the dawn chorus). She whispers, eyes wide with wonder: 'Oh? What IS that?'. Pull back: the cozy red barn interior, the silhouettes of Mo with his straw hat hanging nearby and Bo with her round white glasses folded next to her nest, asleep. She tiptoes off her straw nest, ears tilted toward the sound, golden hour light just starting to slip through the round window.
